oscar.best_picture.note: Spotlight wins Best Picture from only two total Oscars on the night (also Best Original Screenplay) — the fewest total wins by a Best Picture winner since The Greatest Show on Earth (1952). Mad Max: Fury Road took six craft Oscars but no top-line awards. Ceremony took place during the second consecutive #OscarsSoWhite — all 20 acting nominees were white, prompting a high-profile boycott and AMPAS membership reforms in the months that followed.
oscar.best_picture.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Iñárritu wins Best Director in consecutive ceremonies (Birdman, 2015; The Revenant, 2016) — the first back-to-back Best Director wins since Joseph L. Mankiewicz (A Letter to Three Wives, 1950; All About Eve, 1951) and only the third in Oscar history (also John Ford, 1940-1941).
oscar.best_director.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Leonardo DiCaprio's first Oscar after six prior acting nominations — a long-anticipated win that became one of the most-watched moments of the broadcast.
oscar.best_actor.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Brie Larson's first Oscar nomination and win, at age 26.
oscar.best_actress.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Mark Rylance's first Oscar — a notable upset over odds-on favourite Sylvester Stallone (Creed).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: CSV recipients_json contains a parser artifact 'the book' as a fourth recipient (extracted from the 'based on the book by Michael Lewis' clause). Canonical credit is Randolph + McKay. Same root cause as the cer 93 'the play' artifact — see known-issue oscar-adapted-screenplay-the-play-artifact-2026-05-03 (scope expanded to cover this row).
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.note: Emmanuel Lubezki wins Best Cinematography for the third consecutive year (Gravity, 2014; Birdman, 2015; The Revenant, 2016) — the first cinematographer to achieve a three-peat.
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Ennio Morricone, 87, wins his first competitive Oscar after five prior nominations spanning 38 years and an Honorary Oscar in 2007. At the time, the oldest competitive Oscar winner in any category.
oscar.best_original_score.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Second consecutive James Bond theme to win Best Original Song (after Adele's 'Skyfall', 2013).
oscar.best_original_song.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Hungary's second International Feature win (after Mephisto, 1982).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_production_design.note: CSV recipients_json contains role-label artifacts ('Production Design', 'Set Decoration') captured as recipients alongside the canonical Colin Gibson + Lisa Thompson credits. See known-issue oscar-production-design-role-label-artifact-2026-05-03.
oscar.best_production_design.source: 88th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Moonlight wins Best Picture in a televised correction after La La Land was announced in error from a misdelivered envelope — one of the most-replayed live broadcast moments in Oscars history. Moonlight is the first film with an all-Black principal cast, the first LGBTQ-led drama, and the first film distributed by A24 to win Best Picture; also the lowest-budget Best Picture winner of the modern era ($1.5M production budget).
oscar.best_picture.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Damien Chazelle, 32, becomes the youngest Best Director winner in Oscar history (previous record: Norman Taurog, 32 for Skippy, 1931, but Chazelle is months younger). Picture/Director split — La La Land took Director, Moonlight took Picture.
oscar.best_director.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Emma Stone's first Oscar nomination win in the lead acting category (and second overall nomination).
oscar.best_actress.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Mahershala Ali becomes the first Muslim actor to win an acting Oscar.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Viola Davis's first Oscar; she becomes the first Black performer to achieve the Triple Crown of Acting (Oscar, Emmy, Tony). Subsequently completed an EGOT in 2023 with a Grammy win.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Iran's second International Feature win and Asghar Farhadi's second (after A Separation, 2012). Farhadi boycotted the ceremony in protest of the U.S. travel ban affecting Iranian nationals; his statement was read at the podium during acceptance.
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_production_design.note: CSV recipients_json contains role-label artifacts ('Production Design', 'Set Decoration') captured as recipients alongside the canonical David Wasco + Sandy Reynolds-Wasco credits. See known-issue oscar-production-design-role-label-artifact-2026-05-03.
oscar.best_production_design.source: 89th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: The Shape of Water wins four Oscars (Picture, Director, Production Design, Score) from 13 nominations — the most-nominated film of the ceremony. The 90th-anniversary ceremony.
oscar.best_picture.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Guillermo del Toro becomes the third Mexican director to win Best Director in five years (after Cuarón, 2014; Iñárritu, 2015 and 2016) — a remarkable run for the 'Three Amigos' generation of Mexican cinema.
oscar.best_director.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Gary Oldman's first Oscar after his sole prior nomination (Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy, 2012).
oscar.best_actor.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Frances McDormand's second Best Actress Oscar (after Fargo, 1997). Her acceptance speech ended with the call 'Inclusion rider', popularising contractual diversity provisions.
oscar.best_actress.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Sam Rockwell's first Oscar nomination and win.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Allison Janney's first Oscar nomination and win.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Jordan Peele becomes the first Black writer to win Best Original Screenplay.
oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: James Ivory, 89, becomes the oldest Oscar winner in any competitive screenplay category at the time of his win.
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.note: Roger Deakins's first Oscar after fourteen prior nominations — at the time, the most nominations without a win in the category before finally winning.
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Alexandre Desplat's second Best Original Score (after The Grand Budapest Hotel, 2015).
oscar.best_original_score.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Kristen Anderson-Lopez and Robert Lopez's second Best Original Song (after 'Let It Go' from Frozen, 2014). Robert Lopez was already an EGOT recipient before this win.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Chile's first International Feature Oscar, and the first International Feature win for a film with a transgender lead performance (Daniela Vega).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 90th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Green Book wins Best Picture in a divisive ceremony marked by extensive critical and family-of-subjects pushback over the film's portrayal of Don Shirley. The win is widely cited as a Picture/Director split favouring the picture-presentation tradition over Roma's auteur ambition. First ceremony to be hosted-less in 30 years.
oscar.best_picture.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Alfonso Cuarón's second Best Director (after Gravity, 2014); Cuarón also won Best Cinematography (himself) and Best International Feature for Roma — three Oscars across three categories for the same film at the same ceremony, an exceptionally rare achievement. Picture/Director split — Roma took Director + International + Cinematography, Green Book took Picture.
oscar.best_director.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Rami Malek's first Oscar nomination and win — first actor of Egyptian descent to win Best Actor.
oscar.best_actor.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Olivia Colman's first Oscar — a notable upset over odds-on favourite Glenn Close (The Wife, her seventh nomination).
oscar.best_actress.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Mahershala Ali's second Best Supporting Actor (after Moonlight, 2017) — the first Black actor to win two acting Oscars and the second man to win Best Supporting Actor twice in three years (after Christoph Waltz, 2010 and 2013).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: Spike Lee's first competitive Oscar after his 2016 Honorary Award — one of the longest-anticipated competitive wins in modern Oscar history.
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.note: Alfonso Cuarón shoots his own film and wins Best Cinematography — the first time a director has won the cinematography Oscar for directing his own film. Roma is also the first Netflix-distributed film to win major Oscars.
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Ludwig Göransson's first Oscar — also the first time a Marvel Cinematic Universe film won an Oscar in any category. Black Panther won three Oscars at this ceremony (Score, Costume Design, Production Design).
oscar.best_original_score.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Lady Gaga's first Oscar; the song reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100, a rare crossover commercial success for a Best Original Song winner.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse becomes the first non-Disney/Pixar film to win Best Animated Feature in five years (since The LEGO Movie did not win in 2015 — actual prior non-Disney/Pixar winner was Rango, 2012).
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_costume_design.note: Ruth E. Carter becomes the first Black designer to win Best Costume Design.
oscar.best_costume_design.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_production_design.note: Hannah Beachler becomes the first Black designer to win Best Production Design.
oscar.best_production_design.source: 91st Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Parasite becomes the first non-English-language film to win Best Picture in the 92-year history of the Oscars, and the first South Korean film to win in any category. It wins four Oscars in total (Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, International Feature) — Bong Joon-ho personally takes three of them.
oscar.best_picture.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Bong Joon-ho becomes the first Korean filmmaker to win Best Director, and the second Asian director to win after Ang Lee.
oscar.best_director.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Joaquin Phoenix's first Oscar after four prior nominations. Second consecutive Best Actor win for portraying the Joker character (after Heath Ledger's posthumous Best Supporting Actor for The Dark Knight, 2009).
oscar.best_actor.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Renée Zellweger's second Oscar (after Best Supporting Actress for Cold Mountain, 2004) and her first in the lead acting category.
oscar.best_actress.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.winner_film: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Brad Pitt's first acting Oscar (he previously won Best Picture as producer of 12 Years a Slave, 2014).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Laura Dern's first Oscar after two prior nominations spanning 28 years.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: First Best Original Screenplay win for a non-English-language film.
oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: Taika Waititi becomes the first Māori filmmaker to win an Oscar in any writing category.
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.note: Roger Deakins's second Best Cinematography (after Blade Runner 2049, 2018) — won for his single-take aesthetic on 1917.
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Hildur Guðnadóttir becomes the first solo woman to win Best Original Score in the 86-year history of the category. (Anne Dudley won in 1998 as part of an ensemble; Rachel Portman and Anne Dudley earlier wins were in the now-retired Comedy/Musical Score category.)
oscar.best_original_score.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Elton John's second Best Original Song Oscar (after 'Can You Feel the Love Tonight' from The Lion King, 1995). Bernie Taupin's first Oscar.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: South Korea's first International Feature Oscar. First film ever to win both Best Picture and Best International Feature at the same ceremony — a precedent only matched once again (CODA was not an International nominee). The category was renamed from 'Best Foreign Language Film' to 'Best International Feature Film' at this ceremony, making Parasite the first winner under the new category name.
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_makeup_and_hairstyling.note: First ceremony at which Best Makeup and Hairstyling expanded from 3 nominees to 5 — see oscar-expectations.json era_override.
oscar.best_makeup_and_hairstyling.source: 92nd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Frances McDormand becomes only the second person (after Warren Beatty) to be credited as both producer and Best Actress winner for the same film at the same ceremony.
oscar.best_picture.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Chloé Zhao becomes the first woman of colour and the second woman ever (after Kathryn Bigelow) to win Best Director.
oscar.best_director.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Anthony Hopkins, 83, becomes the oldest performer to win an acting Oscar; accepted in absentia in an upset over the late Chadwick Boseman.
oscar.best_actor.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: McDormand's third Best Actress win (after Fargo, 1997; Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri, 2018), joining Meryl Streep, Ingrid Bergman, and Katharine Hepburn (4) as the only actresses with three or more lead-acting Oscars.
oscar.best_actress.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Yuh-jung Youn becomes the first Korean performer to win an acting Oscar and the second Asian woman (after Miyoshi Umeki, 1958) to win Best Supporting Actress.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.note: CSV recipients_json contains a parser artifact 'the play' as a third recipient (extracted from the 'based on the play by Florian Zeller' clause in the Wikipedia source). Canonical credit is Hampton + Zeller. See known-issue oscar-adapted-screenplay-the-play-artifact-2026-05-03.
oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS
oscar.best_original_score.notes: Three-credit team kept as one row (split_co_recipients=false on Best Original Score). winner_recipient singular omitted; winner_recipients array carries canonical credit.

oscar.best_original_song.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Pixar's eleventh Best Animated Feature win and Pete Docter's third (after Up, 2010; Inside Out, 2016).
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Denmark's fourth International Feature win and Thomas Vinterberg's first Oscar (also nominated for Best Director — rare double nomination for an international film director).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_documentary_feature.source: 93rd Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: CODA becomes the first film distributed by a streaming service (Apple TV+) to win Best Picture, and the first Best Picture winner with a predominantly deaf principal cast.
oscar.best_picture.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Picture/Director split — CODA took Best Picture while Campion took Best Director. Campion is only the third woman ever to win Best Director, and consecutive ceremonies producing female Best Director winners (Zhao 2021, Campion 2022) is a first.
oscar.best_director.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Will Smith's first Best Actor win after three nominations. The ceremony was overshadowed by an off-screen incident earlier in the broadcast; Smith subsequently resigned from the Academy and received a 10-year ban from attending Academy events.
oscar.best_actor.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Troy Kotsur becomes the first deaf man to win an acting Oscar (Marlee Matlin, also in CODA, was the first deaf actress, in 1986).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Ariana DeBose, an Afro-Latina openly queer woman, wins for the same role (Anita) for which Rita Moreno won the same Oscar in 1962 — the first time two performers have won an acting Oscar for the same role.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.note: Kenneth Branagh wins his first Oscar after eight nominations across seven categories (acting, directing, picture, screenplay, short film) — the most categories any individual has ever been nominated in.
oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Hans Zimmer's second Best Original Score Oscar, 27 years after his first (The Lion King, 1995).
oscar.best_original_score.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Billie Eilish and Finneas O'Connell become the youngest sibling pair to win an Oscar; third consecutive James Bond theme to win Best Original Song (after 'Skyfall', 2013; 'Writing's on the Wall', 2016).
oscar.best_original_song.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Japan's fifth International Feature win (first since Departures, 2009).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 94th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Everything Everywhere All at Once wins seven Oscars from 11 nominations — the most for any film at this ceremony — including a sweep of four acting/directing categories. A24's first Best Picture win.
oscar.best_picture.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Daniels (Kwan & Scheinert) become the third directing duo to win Best Director (after Robert Wise & Jerome Robbins for West Side Story, 1962; Joel & Ethan Coen for No Country for Old Men, 2008). Kept as one row per category split_co_recipients=false.
oscar.best_director.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Brendan Fraser's first Oscar nomination and win — a high-profile career comeback after roughly a decade out of leading roles.
oscar.best_actor.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Michelle Yeoh becomes the first Asian woman and the first Malaysian-born performer to win Best Actress, and only the second woman of colour ever to win the category (after Halle Berry, 2002).
oscar.best_actress.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Ke Huy Quan's first Oscar — a comeback win 38 years after his child-actor breakout in Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom (1984) and The Goonies (1985).
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Jamie Lee Curtis's first Oscar nomination and win.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.winner_film: All Quiet on the Western Front
oscar.best_cinematography.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.winner_film: All Quiet on the Western Front
oscar.best_original_score.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: 'Naatu Naatu' from RRR is the first song from an Indian production to win Best Original Song, and the first Telugu-language song to win.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio wins Best Animated Feature; del Toro becomes one of a small handful of directors to win Oscars in both live-action (Best Director, The Shape of Water, 2018) and animation categories.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_short.winner_film: The Boy, the Mole, the Fox and the Horse
oscar.best_animated_short.note: CSV recipients_json contains only Charlie Mackesy; canonical credit also includes Matthew Freud (and the production team). The under-extraction is a Wikipedia parser limitation, not a missing winner. See known-issue oscar-animated-short-recipient-undercount-2026-05-03.
oscar.best_animated_short.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.winner_film: All Quiet on the Western Front
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 95th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Oppenheimer wins seven Oscars from 13 nominations — the most for any film at this ceremony.
oscar.best_picture.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Christopher Nolan's first competitive Oscar after eight prior nominations across directing, screenplay, and picture categories.
oscar.best_director.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Cillian Murphy's first Oscar nomination and win — the first Irish-born actor to win Best Actor.
oscar.best_actor.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Emma Stone's second Best Actress Oscar (after La La Land, 2017), at age 35.
oscar.best_actress.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Robert Downey Jr.'s first Oscar after three nominations spanning four decades — a high-profile late-career win following a public reinvention.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Ludwig Göransson's second Oscar (after Black Panther, 2019).
oscar.best_original_score.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.note: Billie Eilish and Finneas O'Connell's second Best Original Song Oscar (after 'No Time to Die', 2022) — the youngest two-time Oscar winners in any category.
oscar.best_original_song.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Hayao Miyazaki's second Best Animated Feature Oscar (after Spirited Away, 2003) — at age 83, the oldest winner in the category. Studio Ghibli's first win in 21 years.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: United Kingdom's first International Feature win in the modern era of the category.
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.note: First Japanese-produced film to win Best Visual Effects, and Toho Studios' first VFX Oscar — a notable upset over Hollywood blockbuster competition on a far smaller budget.
oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 96th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_picture.note: Anora wins five Oscars from six nominations — a clean sweep of Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, Editing, and Actress for an indie film. Neon's first Best Picture win.
oscar.best_picture.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_director.note: Sean Baker becomes the first individual to win four Oscars in a single ceremony for the same film (Picture, Director, Original Screenplay, Film Editing) — a feat last approached by Walt Disney's multi-category wins for short films.
oscar.best_director.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actor.note: Adrien Brody's second Best Actor Oscar (after The Pianist, 2003) — 22 years after his first, when at 29 he was the youngest Best Actor winner.
oscar.best_actor.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_actress.note: Mikey Madison's first Oscar nomination and win — a high-profile upset over odds-on favourite Demi Moore (The Substance).
oscar.best_actress.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actor.note: Kieran Culkin's first Oscar nomination and win.
oscar.best_supporting_actor.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_supporting_actress.note: Zoe Saldaña's first Oscar nomination and win — the first American of Dominican descent to win an acting Oscar.
oscar.best_supporting_actress.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_screenplay.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_adapted_screenplay.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_cinematography.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_film_editing.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_score.note: Daniel Blumberg's first Oscar nomination and win — composing his first feature score.
oscar.best_original_score.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_original_song.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_animated_feature.note: Flow becomes the first Latvian film to win an Oscar and the first independently produced animated feature (no major studio backing) to win Best Animated Feature; produced in the free open-source software Blender.
oscar.best_animated_feature.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_international_feature.note: Brazil's first International Feature Oscar — a notable upset over Emilia Pérez (which entered the ceremony with 13 nominations, the most ever for a non-English-language film).
oscar.best_international_feature.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_makeup_and_hairstyling.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS

oscar.best_visual_effects.source: 97th Academy Awards Wikipedia article / Variety / AMPAS
